title: The Summer Greenhouse
introKo:
Rose 부인이 여름맞이로 유리 온실을 지었어요. 안은 한여름보다 더 덥다네요.
이번에도 아주 건전한 원예 이야기예요. …표면적으로는요.

quiz.q: Rose 부인이 이번 여름을 위해 새로 만든 것은?

normal.text: Summer arrives in the village. It is the hottest summer in years, and your neighbor, Mrs. Rose, has big news.
She calls you over the fence, past her famous eggplants. "Come and see!" she says. "I built a greenhouse, just for the summer."
Inside, the air is warm and wet. "It gets very hot in here," she says. "I hope you don't mind. I need your strong hands again."

options.en: I don't mind at all. I like the heat.
options.reaction: "Good," she smiles. "Then you will love it in here."

options.en: Wow, it really is warm in here.
options.reaction: "That is my secret," she says. "Warm and wet — everything grows faster that way."

normal.text: First, you plant tomato seeds. "Push each one in slowly," she says. "The soil is soft and ready. It has waited all spring for this."
Next, the young tomato plants need support. "Hold the stick firm," she says. "Every plant needs something strong to hold on to."

options.en: Is this firm enough for you?
options.reaction: "Oh yes," she says. "Very firm. You have learned a lot since spring."
"Gently, dear. Tight enough to hold, but loose enough to breathe. That is the trick with everything."

normal.text: Now you spray water on every plant. "In this heat, they get thirsty fast," she says. "Don't stop until they drip."
Soon the glass is covered with steam. "It always does that," Mrs. Rose laughs, "when things heat up in here."
In the warmest corner, her melons rest on soft straw. "They love the heat. Look how round they are. Go on — feel how heavy they have grown."

options.reaction: "The heat does that," she says softly. "Everything ripens faster in here."
"Patience, dear," she says. "The sweetest ones are always worth the wait."

normal.text: You step out into the cool evening air. Your shirt is damp, your basket is full. "Same time next week?" she asks. "The tomatoes will be red by then."
That night, you leave your window open. Summer has only just started, you think — and it is going to be a long, hot one.

hard.text: Summer settles over the village — the hottest anyone can remember — and your neighbor, Mrs. Rose, has been building something new.
She waves you over the fence with a proud smile, past her famous eggplants. "Come and see what I built," she says. "A greenhouse, all glass, just for the summer."
Inside, the air is thick and warm. "It gets terribly hot in here," she says. "I do hope you can handle the heat, because I need your strong hands again."

options.en: I don't mind at all. I have always liked the heat.
options.ko: 전혀 상관없어요. 원래 더운 걸 좋아해요.
options.reaction: "Good," she smiles. "Then you are going to love it in here."

options.en: Wow, it really is warm in here.
options.reaction: "That is my little secret," she says. "Warm and wet — everything grows so much faster that way."

hard.text: Your first task is planting tomato seeds. "Push each one in slowly," she instructs. "The soil is soft and more than ready — it has waited all spring for this moment."
Next come the young tomato plants, which have grown tall and heavy; they need support. "Hold the stick firm," she says. "Every plant needs something strong to hold on to."

options.en: Is this firm enough for you?
options.reaction: "Oh yes," she says. "Wonderfully firm. You have learned a great deal since spring."

options.en: Should I tie them up tight?
options.reaction: "Gently, dear. Tight enough to hold them, loose enough to let them breathe. That is the trick with everything."

hard.text: Then you spray a fine mist over every plant. "In this heat they get thirsty so quickly," she says. "Don't you dare stop until every leaf is dripping."
Before long, the glass walls are covered in steam. "It always does that," Mrs. Rose laughs, "whenever things start heating up in here."
In the warmest corner, her prized melons rest on a bed of soft straw. "They adore the heat. Look how round they have become. Go on — feel how heavy they have grown."

options.reaction: "The heat does that," she says softly. "Everything ripens so much faster in here."

options.en: When exactly will they be ripe?
options.reaction: "Patience, dear," she says. "The sweetest ones are always the ones worth waiting for."

hard.text: You step out into the cool evening air, shirt damp and basket full. "Same time next week?" she asks. "The tomatoes should be blushing red by then."
That night you leave your window wide open. Summer has only just begun, you think — and something tells you it is going to be a long, hot one.

wink.greenhouse: 온실은 문을 닫는 순간 뜨겁고 습해지는 유리 공간이에요. 이 이야기 내내 두 사람이 그 안에 함께 있다는 점이 포인트예요.
wink.hot: 덥다는 뜻이 맞긴 한데, 영어에서 hot은 사람이나 분위기가 '뜨겁다'는 의미로도 아주 자주 쓰여요. "I like the heat"도 두 가지로 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.push: "Push each one in slowly"는 씨앗 심기 요령으로는 완벽한 지침이에요. 문장만 따로 떼어 소리 내어 읽지 않는 게 좋을 뿐이죠.
wink.firm: 지지대가 단단해야 하는 건 원예 상식이지만, "Is this firm enough for you?"를 진지한 얼굴로 말하기는 쉽지 않은 문장이에요.
wink.thirsty: 목마른 게 식물만은 아니에요. 요즘 영어에서 thirsty는 뭔가를 간절히 바라는 사람을 놀릴 때도 쓰는 단어예요.
wink.steam: 유리에 김이 서리는 건 과학 현상이지만, 영어에서는 분위기가 달아오른 장면을 '김이 서렸다'고 표현하기도 해요.
wink.drip: 잎에서 물이 떨어질 때까지 주라는 성실한 지침인데, 소리 내어 읽으면 어쩐지 목소리가 낮아지는 문장이에요.
wink.ripens: "Everything ripens faster in here" — 이 온실에서 빨리 무르익는 게 과일만은 아닐지도 몰라요.
wink.feel: 잘 자랐는지 만져 보라는 평범한 권유예요. 대상이 하필 멜론이라는 게 문제일 뿐이죠. Mrs. Rose의 정원 이야기를 읽었다면 이유를 아실 거예요.
wink.damp: 저녁에 셔츠가 축축해진 건 순전히 온실 노동 때문이에요. 아마도요.

introEn:
Mrs. Rose built a glass greenhouse for summer — hotter than July inside.
Perfectly wholesome gardening. ...on the surface.
